_comment: Language-NEUTRAL structured-data facts, single source of truth for the index + competition + workshop + contact JSON-LD. Values are copied verbatim from the live JSON-LD blocks; do not 'improve' them. Translatable strings (names/descriptions/labels) live in _data/i18n/<lang>.json.
_commentEventDates: startDate and eventPublishStartDate MUST be kept identical. eleventyComputed.js emits the index/competition Event using eventPublishStartDate; startDate is the neutral fact and the proxy guard. Changing one without the other silently publishes a stale date. Adding eventPublishStartDate is what OPENS the index/competition Event gate; removing it re-closes it.
_commentWorkshop: Reference data for the workshop Event JSON-LD, which is DATE-GATED: eleventyComputed.js emits the workshop Event only once a real `workshopStartDate` (and optionally `workshopEndDate`) is added here — until then the workshop page carries only Organization + BreadcrumbList. (An undated Event, and its undated/locationless subEvents, are invalid rich results, so the placeholder talk/panel subEvents were dropped; re-add only REAL dated subEvents on restore.) The fields below stay as the neutral facts for that future restore: own attendance mode (Offline, vs the index/competition Mixed); `organizer` Hamburg + CMU; the panel `performer` list (proper nouns + affiliations). Translatable strings (event name/description, the former talk/panel subEvent names) live in i18n/<lang>.json jsonld.*.
_commentOpenDayHamburg: Neutral facts for the Hamburg Open Day Event JSON-LD on open-day-hamburg.html, DATE-GATED on openDayHamburgStartDate in the same way as the workshop gate — but unlike the workshop and the index/competition Events, this one has a REAL date and a REAL venue, so the gate is open and a valid Event is emitted today. This is independent of the index/competition Event gate (eventPublishStartDate), which opened on 2026-07-22 when the revised schedule was locked — do not couple them. Talk-level subEvents are deliberately omitted: talk titles are TBA, and an Event node without a real name/startDate/location is an invalid rich result (same reasoning that dropped the workshop's placeholder subEvents). Add real dated subEvents only once titles are confirmed. Translatable strings (event name/description, breadcrumb) live in i18n/<lang>.json jsonld.*.
